content.text: LLMs generate blog posts and marketing copy at a quality level that makes them practical in production pipelines — not as a replacement for human writers, but as a force multiplier that handles first drafts, variant generation, and format adaptation. The core challenge is controlling output precisely: getting the right length, structure, tone, and factual accuracy for each content type, and building pipelines that are repeatable and evaluable rather than one-off prompt experiments.
Content Generation Pipeline

attrs.alt: Content Generation Pipeline with 4 stages: (1) Content Brief — topic, audience, tone, length, key points, CTA, SEO keywords; (2) Outline Generation — LLM creates H1/H2 structure, hook type, CTA placement, with human review; (3) Section Drafting — intro, body sections, conclusion, meta description, social snippets drafted in parallel; (4) Refinement Passes — style conformance, fact check, readability, SEO density, CTA strength, title variants, A/B copy variants. Five copy formats with distinct requirements: Blog Post (800-2500 words, scannable H2/H3), Product Description (feature→benefit, 80-300 words), Email Copy (subject line, preview text, one CTA), Ad Copy (30-char headline, A/B variants), Landing Page (hero, social proof, FAQ, above-fold CTA). Four quality dimensions: Accuracy, Originality, Engagement, Brand Conformance.

content.text: 1. Structured Blog Post Generation

Generating a full blog post in one prompt produces mediocre results: the model has no opportunity to plan the structure before writing, and the user has no chance to redirect it before hundreds of tokens are committed to the wrong angle. The brief → outline → draft pipeline separates structure decisions from prose decisions, and inserts a human review point between them.

import anthropic
import json
import asyncio
from dataclasses import dataclass, field

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

@dataclass
class ContentBrief:
    topic: str
    audience: str
    tone: str                        # e.g. "educational and direct"
    target_word_count: int
    key_points: list[str]
    cta: str                         # desired call to action
    seo_keywords: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    avoid: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # topics/phrases to exclude


@dataclass
class BlogOutline:
    title: str
    meta_description: str
    hook_type: str          # "question" | "statistic" | "anecdote" | "bold_claim"
    sections: list[dict]    # [{heading, key_points: [str], approx_words: int}]
    cta_placement: str      # "end" | "mid" | "both"


def generate_outline(brief: ContentBrief) -> BlogOutline:
    """
    Generate a blog post outline from a content brief.
    Human reviews and optionally edits this before drafting.
    """
    points_str = "\n".join(f"  - {p}" for p in brief.key_points)
    kws_str = ", ".join(brief.seo_keywords) if brief.seo_keywords else "none"
    avoid_str = ", ".join(brief.avoid) if brief.avoid else "none"

    prompt = (
        f"Create a detailed blog post outline for the following brief.\n\n"
        f"Topic: {brief.topic}\n"
        f"Target audience: {brief.audience}\n"
        f"Tone: {brief.tone}\n"
        f"Target length: {brief.target_word_count} words\n"
        f"Key points to cover:\n{points_str}\n"
        f"CTA: {brief.cta}\n"
        f"SEO keywords (use naturally): {kws_str}\n"
        f"Avoid: {avoid_str}\n\n"
        "Return JSON with:\n"
        '"title": string (compelling H1, includes primary keyword if given)\n'
        '"meta_description": string (150–160 chars, includes primary keyword)\n'
        '"hook_type": "question" | "statistic" | "anecdote" | "bold_claim"\n'
        '"sections": [{"heading": str, "key_points": [str], "approx_words": int}]\n'
        '"cta_placement": "end" | "mid" | "both"\n'
        "Return only JSON."
    )
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
    )
    data = json.loads(response.content[0].text)
    return BlogOutline(**data)
Parallel Section Drafting

async def draft_section_async(
    section: dict,
    brief: ContentBrief,
    outline: BlogOutline,
    position: str,   # "intro" | "body" | "conclusion"
) -> tuple[str, str]:  # (heading, text)
    """
    Draft one section of the blog post.
    Returns (heading, drafted_text).
    """
    points_str = "\n".join(f"- {p}" for p in section["key_points"])
    kws_str = ", ".join(outline.meta_description.split()[:5])  # approximate keyword hint

    system = (
        f"You are a {brief.tone} writer producing a blog post titled '{outline.title}'. "
        f"The target audience is {brief.audience}. "
        "Write clearly and concisely. Use active voice. Avoid filler phrases and clichés. "
        "Include specific, concrete details. Do not repeat information from other sections."
    )
    position_instructions = {
        "intro": (
            f"Write the introduction ({section['approx_words']} words). "
            f"Open with a {outline.hook_type} hook. Establish the problem/opportunity clearly. "
            "End with a transition into the first main section."
        ),
        "body": (
            f"Write the section '{section['heading']}' ({section['approx_words']} words). "
            f"Cover these points:\n{points_str}\n"
            "Use concrete examples. Include data or specifics where relevant."
        ),
        "conclusion": (
            f"Write the conclusion ({section['approx_words']} words). "
            "Summarize the core insight. Do not repeat every point. "
            f"End with this CTA: {brief.cta}"
        ),
    }[position]

    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    result = await loop.run_in_executor(
        None,
        lambda: client.messages.create(
            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
            max_tokens=section["approx_words"] * 2,  # generous headroom
            system=system,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": position_instructions}],
        ).content[0].text,
    )
    return section["heading"], result


async def draft_blog_post(brief: ContentBrief, outline: BlogOutline) -> str:
    """
    Draft all sections in parallel and assemble the full post.
    """
    tasks = []
    for i, section in enumerate(outline.sections):
        if i == 0:
            position = "intro"
        elif i == len(outline.sections) - 1:
            position = "conclusion"
        else:
            position = "body"
        tasks.append(draft_section_async(section, brief, outline, position))

    sections = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)

    # Assemble in order
    post_parts = [f"# {outline.title}\n"]
    for heading, text in sections:
        if heading.lower() not in ("introduction", "intro"):
            post_parts.append(f"\n## {heading}\n")
        post_parts.append(text)

    return "\n".join(post_parts)
2. Marketing Copy by Format
Product Descriptions

Product descriptions must bridge features (what the product does) and benefits (why the customer should care). The prompt must prevent the model from defaulting to generic enthusiasm ("amazing", "incredible") and instead produce copy grounded in specific product attributes.

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Product:
    name: str
    category: str
    features: list[str]          # factual feature list
    target_audience: str
    primary_use_case: str
    price_tier: str              # "budget" | "mid" | "premium" | "luxury"
    differentiators: list[str]   # what makes this product distinct


def generate_product_description(
    product: Product,
    word_count: int = 150,
    format: str = "prose",       # "prose" | "bullets" | "hybrid"
    channel: str = "ecommerce",  # "ecommerce" | "print" | "social"
) -> str:
    features_str = "\n".join(f"- {f}" for f in product.features)
    diff_str = "\n".join(f"- {d}" for d in product.differentiators)

    format_instructions = {
        "prose":   f"Write {word_count} words of flowing prose.",
        "bullets": f"Write a 1-sentence intro, then {min(6, len(product.features))} benefit-focused bullet points.",
        "hybrid":  f"Write 40 words of prose, then 4–5 bullet points. Total ~{word_count} words.",
    }[format]

    channel_notes = {
        "ecommerce": "Include the primary SEO keyword naturally. Start with the key benefit.",
        "print":     "No SEO constraints. Focus on sensory language and aspiration.",
        "social":    "Punchy and scannable. Use second person ('you'). One strong hook.",
    }[channel]

    prompt = (
        f"Write a product description for: {product.name}\n"
        f"Category: {product.category}\n"
        f"Target audience: {product.target_audience}\n"
        f"Primary use case: {product.primary_use_case}\n"
        f"Price tier: {product.price_tier}\n"
        f"Features (convert to benefits in the copy):\n{features_str}\n"
        f"Key differentiators:\n{diff_str}\n\n"
        f"Format: {format_instructions}\n"
        f"Channel: {channel_notes}\n"
        "Rules: No generic superlatives (amazing, incredible, revolutionary). "
        "Every claim must be implied by the features above. "
        "Return only the product description text."
    )
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=512,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
    )
    return response.content[0].text


# Example
cooler = Product(
    name="IcePeak Pro 45L",
    category="Outdoor coolers",
    features=["Rotomolded LLDPE body", "3-inch insulated walls", "10-day ice retention", "Bear-resistant certified", "18 kg empty weight"],
    target_audience="Backcountry campers and overlanders",
    primary_use_case="Multi-day wilderness trips without resupply",
    price_tier="premium",
    differentiators=["Longest ice retention in class at 10 days", "Bear canister certification for national park access"],
)
print(generate_product_description(cooler, word_count=120, format="hybrid", channel="ecommerce"))
Email Copy

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class EmailCopySpec:
    campaign_goal: str          # "announce feature", "recover churned user", "upsell"
    audience_segment: str
    key_message: str            # one sentence: what is this email about?
    cta_text: str               # button text
    cta_destination: str        # brief description of landing destination
    sender_name: str
    personalization_fields: list[str] = None  # e.g. ["first_name", "company"]


@dataclass
class EmailCopy:
    subject_lines: list[str]    # 3–5 options for A/B testing
    preview_texts: list[str]    # matching preview text for each subject
    body: str
    cta_text: str


def generate_email_copy(spec: EmailCopySpec) -> EmailCopy:
    pers_str = ", ".join(f"{{{{{f}}}}}" for f in (spec.personalization_fields or []))
    prompt = (
        f"Write marketing email copy for:\n"
        f"Campaign goal: {spec.campaign_goal}\n"
        f"Audience: {spec.audience_segment}\n"
        f"Key message: {spec.key_message}\n"
        f"CTA: '{spec.cta_text}' (links to: {spec.cta_destination})\n"
        f"From: {spec.sender_name}\n"
        + (f"Available personalization: {pers_str}\n" if pers_str else "")
        + "\nReturn JSON with:\n"
        '"subject_lines": list of 4 subject lines (vary approach: curiosity, benefit, urgency, question)\n'
        '"preview_texts": list of matching preview texts (90 chars max each)\n'
        '"body": email body (150–250 words, conversational, single focused message, no headers)\n'
        '"cta_text": final CTA button text (may differ from input if a better option exists)\n'
        "Rules: no 'Dear [name]' opener, no spam trigger words ('free', 'urgent', 'act now'), "
        "use personalization tokens where natural. Return only JSON."
    )
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
    )
    data = json.loads(response.content[0].text)
    return EmailCopy(**data)


spec = EmailCopySpec(
    campaign_goal="Announce new AI writing assistant feature to existing subscribers",
    audience_segment="Newsletter subscribers who opened last 3 emails",
    key_message="The new AI writing assistant drafts your first version in seconds",
    cta_text="Try it now",
    cta_destination="product feature page with 1-click demo",
    sender_name="Maya at Horizon",
    personalization_fields=["first_name"],
)
email = generate_email_copy(spec)
print("Subject options:", email.subject_lines)
print("Body:\n", email.body)
Ad Copy Variants

@dataclass
class AdCopySpec:
    product_name: str
    value_proposition: str
    audience_pain_point: str
    platform: str               # "google" | "meta" | "linkedin" | "twitter"
    campaign_objective: str     # "awareness" | "consideration" | "conversion"

@dataclass
class AdCopy:
    headlines: list[str]        # 3–5 options
    descriptions: list[str]     # matching descriptions
    hook_type: list[str]        # label per headline: "pain"|"benefit"|"social_proof"|"urgency"

PLATFORM_LIMITS = {
    "google":   {"headline": 30, "description": 90},
    "meta":     {"headline": 40, "description": 125},
    "linkedin": {"headline": 70, "description": 150},
    "twitter":  {"headline": 70, "description": 280},
}


def generate_ad_copy(spec: AdCopySpec, n_variants: int = 4) -> AdCopy:
    limits = PLATFORM_LIMITS.get(spec.platform, PLATFORM_LIMITS["google"])
    prompt = (
        f"Create {n_variants} {spec.platform} ad copy variants for:\n"
        f"Product: {spec.product_name}\n"
        f"Value prop: {spec.value_proposition}\n"
        f"Audience pain point: {spec.audience_pain_point}\n"
        f"Objective: {spec.campaign_objective}\n\n"
        f"Constraints: Headline max {limits['headline']} chars. Description max {limits['description']} chars.\n"
        f"Create {n_variants} variants using different angles: pain-focused, benefit-focused, social proof, urgency.\n"
        "Return JSON with:\n"
        '"headlines": list of strings (within character limit)\n'
        '"descriptions": list of matching descriptions (within character limit)\n'
        '"hook_type": list of strings (label for each: "pain"|"benefit"|"social_proof"|"urgency")\n'
        "Verify every string is within the character limit before returning. Return only JSON."
    )
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
    )
    data = json.loads(response.content[0].text)
    # Enforce limits — model sometimes goes slightly over
    data["headlines"]    = [h[:limits["headline"]]    for h in data["headlines"]]
    data["descriptions"] = [d[:limits["description"]] for d in data["descriptions"]]
    return AdCopy(**data)
3. SEO-Aware Content Generation

Keyword stuffing in the prompt produces keyword-stuffed copy. The right approach is to provide target keywords and instruct the model to use them naturally, then verify keyword presence and density as a post-generation check rather than a generation constraint.
4. A/B Copy Variant Generation

A/B testing requires meaningfully different variants — not just paraphrases. The model should vary one independent element per variant (hook type, emotional appeal, length, CTA wording) while keeping the core message constant, so test results are attributable to specific changes.

from typing import Literal

VariantAxis = Literal[
    "hook",         # vary the opening approach
    "emotional_appeal",  # fear/aspiration/social_proof/authority
    "length",       # short vs detailed
    "cta",          # vary the call to action
    "format",       # prose vs bullets vs numbered
]


def generate_ab_variants(
    base_copy: str,
    axis: VariantAxis,
    n_variants: int = 3,
    context: str = "",
) -> list[dict]:  # [{variant_label, copy, axis_value}]
    axis_instructions = {
        "hook": (
            f"Create {n_variants} variants with different opening approaches: "
            "1) opens with a provocative question, 2) opens with a concrete statistic, "
            "3) opens with a bold declarative statement. Keep everything else the same."
        ),
        "emotional_appeal": (
            f"Create {n_variants} variants that appeal to different emotions: "
            "1) fear of missing out / pain avoidance, 2) aspiration / positive vision, "
            "3) social proof / belonging. Core message unchanged."
        ),
        "length": (
            f"Create {n_variants} length variants from the same message: "
            "1) 50% shorter (cut to essentials only), 2) original length, "
            "3) 50% longer (add supporting evidence). Same structure."
        ),
        "cta": (
            f"Create {n_variants} variants with different CTAs: "
            "1) action-focused ('Start now'), 2) benefit-focused ('Get your free report'), "
            "3) low-friction ('See how it works'). Copy body unchanged."
        ),
        "format": (
            f"Create {n_variants} format variants: "
            "1) flowing prose only, 2) bullet-point list with intro sentence, "
            "3) numbered steps. Same information."
        ),
    }[axis]

    context_block = f"\nContext: {context}" if context else ""
    prompt = (
        f"BASE COPY:{context_block}\n{base_copy}\n\n"
        f"TASK: {axis_instructions}\n\n"
        "Return JSON array of objects, each with:\n"
        '{"variant_label": str, "copy": str, "axis_value": str}\n'
        "Return only the JSON array."
    )
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=2048,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
    )
    return json.loads(response.content[0].text)
5. Content Quality Evaluation

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class ContentQualityReport:
    hook_strength: float          # 0–1: how compelling is the opening?
    clarity_score: float          # 0–1: is the message clear?
    cta_effectiveness: float      # 0–1: is the CTA specific and actionable?
    brand_conformance: float      # 0–1: matches voice guide?
    originality_flags: list[str]  # generic phrases detected
    overall: float
    verdict: str                  # "publish" | "revise" | "rewrite"
    top_issues: list[str]


def evaluate_content(
    content: str,
    content_type: str,   # "blog" | "email" | "product_desc" | "ad"
    brand_voice_summary: str = "",
) -> ContentQualityReport:
    voice_block = f"\nBrand voice: {brand_voice_summary}" if brand_voice_summary else ""
    prompt = (
        f"Evaluate this {content_type} content across four dimensions (0.0–1.0 each).{voice_block}\n\n"
        f"CONTENT:\n{content}\n\n"
        "Score and explain:\n"
        "1. hook_strength: How compelling and specific is the opening?"
        " (1.0 = immediately grabs attention; 0.0 = generic or boring)\n"
        "2. clarity_score: Is the core message clear on first read?"
        " (1.0 = instantly understood; 0.0 = confusing or vague)\n"
        "3. cta_effectiveness: How specific and actionable is the call to action?"
        " (1.0 = crystal clear next step; 0.0 = no CTA or very vague)\n"
        "4. brand_conformance: Does it match the brand voice?"
        " (1.0 = perfect match; 0.0 = off-brand or generic)\n"
        "5. originality_flags: List any generic/clichéd phrases found (empty list if none)\n"
        "6. top_issues: Top 3 specific improvements needed\n"
        "Return JSON: {hook_strength, clarity_score, cta_effectiveness, brand_conformance, originality_flags, top_issues}. "
        "Return only JSON."
    )
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=512,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
    )
    data = json.loads(response.content[0].text)
    overall = (
        data["hook_strength"] * 0.25
        + data["clarity_score"] * 0.30
        + data["cta_effectiveness"] * 0.25
        + data["brand_conformance"] * 0.20
    )
    verdict = "publish" if overall >= 0.80 else ("revise" if overall >= 0.60 else "rewrite")
    return ContentQualityReport(
        hook_strength=data["hook_strength"],
        clarity_score=data["clarity_score"],
        cta_effectiveness=data["cta_effectiveness"],
        brand_conformance=data["brand_conformance"],
        originality_flags=data.get("originality_flags", []),
        overall=round(overall, 3),
        verdict=verdict,
        top_issues=data.get("top_issues", []),
    )
Common Mistakes
One-shot full-post generation.
Asking for a 1500-word article in a single prompt produces a structurally unpredictable result. Separate outline generation from section drafting so structure is validated before prose is written.
No constraints on generic language.

Without explicit prohibitions, LLMs default to superlatives ("amazing", "game-changing", "revolutionary") that trained readers instantly dismiss. Always include a list of forbidden phrases in the system prompt.
Hallucinated statistics in marketing copy.

The model will confidently invent research studies, customer counts, and market statistics. Either provide real data in the prompt ("Use these figures: ...") or explicitly instruct the model not to cite any statistics it was not given.
Identical A/B variants.

Generating "3 different versions" without specifying what should differ produces near-identical paraphrases. Define a specific axis of variation (hook type, emotional appeal, length) for each variant so the test produces actionable signal.
Ignoring character limits in ad copy.

Models frequently produce headlines that exceed Google's 30-character or Meta's 40-character limits. Always validate character counts programmatically after generation and truncate or regenerate overruns.
No human review point in the pipeline.

Fully automated content pipelines that publish without a human checkpoint are high-risk for factual errors, brand violations, and tone missteps. Insert a review stage at outline approval and before final publication.
Best Practices
Invest in the brief, not the prompt.

A well-structured content brief (topic, audience, key points, CTA, forbidden phrases, SEO keywords) produces dramatically better output than a long, improvised prompt. Templatize briefs as dataclass inputs and reuse them.
Draft sections in parallel.
Blog post sections are largely independent. Drafting them concurrently with asyncio reduces total latency from (N × section_time) to approximately max(section_time).
Provide factual grounding when available.

If the product has real customer testimonials, case study numbers, or published research, inject them into the prompt. The model will use real data preferentially over invented data when both are accessible.
Use small models for mechanical copy, larger for nuance.

Short-form copy (email subject lines, ad descriptions, meta descriptions) and structured JSON extraction are well-handled by small models. Landing page hero copy and long-form brand voice writing benefit from larger models.
Automate quality gates before publication.

Run SEO checks and brand conformance scoring programmatically on every generated piece. Route items below threshold to a human review queue rather than rejecting them outright — LLM-generated content often needs a light edit rather than full regeneration.
Key Takeaways

Separate outline generation from section drafting. Structure decisions and prose decisions compete when forced into one prompt; separating them produces better results and allows human course-correction at the outline stage.
Each copy format has distinct structural requirements (word count, CTA placement, character limits) that must be encoded as explicit constraints, not assumed from the format label alone.

Prohibit hallucinated statistics explicitly. Any marketing copy that will be published publicly should either receive real data through the prompt or be instructed not to cite any figures the prompt did not supply.
A/B testing works only when variants differ on a single, well-defined axis. Specify the axis (hook type, emotional appeal, length, CTA wording) explicitly — not just "3 different versions".

Content quality evaluation (hook strength, clarity, CTA effectiveness, brand conformance) can be automated as a model-graded gate that routes borderline output to human review rather than rejecting or blindly publishing.
